{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.3,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.3,
    "R1.4": 0.0,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.2",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "What scope of cleanup do you want? A) Light copyedit only (grammar, clarity, concision; preserve all technical claims and structure), or B) Substantive technical cleanup (resolve contradictions such as “lock-free/wait-free” vs mutex-based operations, tighten progress/linearizability language, and adjust claims/positioning accordingly).",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The text contains apparent contradictions (e.g., lock-free/wait-free claims alongside mutex-based operations) that require logical consistency checks.",
    "R1.6": "“Clean this up” most directly implies sentence- and paragraph-level polish for clarity, concision, and tone.",
    "RX.1": "Any uncertainties or unresolved contradictions should be explicitly flagged rather than silently assumed away.",
    "RX.2": "Overreaching claims (e.g., “strongest progress guarantee in the literature”) must be tempered or supported to avoid overclaiming.",
    "RX.3": "Work should stay within the chosen cleanup scope and avoid expanding into redesign or new experimentation.",
    "RX.4": "Avoiding reinforcement of misleading concurrency guarantees reduces risk of downstream misuse or harm.",
    "RX.5": "Edits should remain traceable to the source text, with clear indication of where claims are softened or qualified."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: What scope of cleanup do you want? A) Light copyedit only (grammar, clarity, concision; preserve all technical claims and structure), or B) Substantive technical cleanup (resolve contradictions such as “lock-free/wait-free” vs mutex-based operations, tighten progress/linearizability language, and adjust claims/positioning accordingly).

rationale.R1.2: The text contains apparent contradictions (e.g., lock-free/wait-free claims alongside mutex-based operations) that require logical consistency checks.
rationale.R1.6: “Clean this up” most directly implies sentence- and paragraph-level polish for clarity, concision, and tone.
rationale.RX.1: Any uncertainties or unresolved contradictions should be explicitly flagged rather than silently assumed away.
rationale.RX.2: Overreaching claims (e.g., “strongest progress guarantee in the literature”) must be tempered or supported to avoid overclaiming.
rationale.RX.3: Work should stay within the chosen cleanup scope and avoid expanding into redesign or new experimentation.
rationale.RX.4: Avoiding reinforcement of misleading concurrency guarantees reduces risk of downstream misuse or harm.
rationale.RX.5: Edits should remain traceable to the source text, with clear indication of where claims are softened or qualified.
